task_completion.description: Open the original janus_29mm.ork file in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket GUI is visible with the 'janus_29mm' rocket design loaded, showing the rocket schematic and component tree.

task_completion.description: Change the material density of the Nose Cone, exterior Body Tubes, and Fin set to 0.65 g/cm³ (650 kg/m³).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Component configuration dialogs (for Nose Cone, Body Tube, or Fins) show the material set to a custom value with a density of exactly 0.65 g/cm³ or 650 kg/m³.

task_completion.description: Remove the mass overrides on the primary structural components so the new material density takes effect.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Override mass' checkbox in the component configuration dialogs for the Nose Cone, Body Tubes, and Fins is unchecked.

task_completion.description: Add a ballast (mass component) of at least 10 grams (0.01 kg) to the nose cone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Mass component' or 'Ballast' item is visible in the component tree as a child of the Nose Cone, and its configuration window shows a mass of >= 10g (0.01 kg).

task_completion.description: Achieve a final stability margin of at least 1.50 calibers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main OpenRocket window displays a stability margin of >= 1.50 cal in the top right corner or the flight configuration summary.

task_completion.description: Save the modified design and export the summary report containing density, ballast mass, and stability margin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the contents of 'lw_pla_report.txt' documenting the 0.65 g/cm³ density, the specific ballast mass added, and the >= 1.50 stability margin. A file manager or terminal shows 'janus_lw_pla.ork' and 'lw_pla_report.txt' in their respective target directories.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the design rather than manually editing the XML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the OpenRocket application interface, component tree, and configuration dialogs.

integrity.description: The report contains actual values derived from the simulation rather than hardcoded target requirements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The stability margin and ballast mass written in the text report exactly match the values visible in the OpenRocket GUI screenshots.

integrity.description: Internal motor mounts and recovery gear densities were not modified to cheat the stability margin.
integrity.visual_evidence: The component tree does not show unexpected modifications to internal tubes, centering rings, or parachutes, and their configuration windows (if opened) show default materials.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target density for LW-PLA is 650 kg/m³ (0.65 g/cm³) with a tolerance of 20 kg/m³. To achieve the required stability margin of >= 1.50 calibers, the agent MUST uncheck the 'Override mass' settings on the primary structural components (Nose Cone, Body Tubes, Fins) because the original file has hardcoded masses. The agent must also add a ballast (mass component) of at least 0.01 kg (10 grams) inside the nose cone. The final files must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/janus_lw_pla.ork and /home/ga/Documents/exports/lw_pla_report.txt.
